fixes_from_round_1.critical_composition_md: ADEQUATE — Decision 7 explicitly maps composition.md → grid-techniques.md with rationale. File is created in Task 4 Files to modify and listed in Dependencies.
fixes_from_round_1.major_library_catalog_md: ADEQUATE — Task 1 Files to modify now includes shared/design-references/library-catalog.md. Architecture lists it. Acceptance Criteria includes a verification line.
fixes_from_round_1.minor_color_theory_naming: ADEQUATE — Decision 7 covers both mappings: color-theory.md → color-psychology.md + color-principles.md, and composition.md → grid-techniques.md.
fixes_from_round_1.minor_edge_cases_by_design: ADEQUATE — Task header comment documents that tasks are brief scope descriptions; edge cases live in Testing Strategy. BY DESIGN is correctly applied.
fixes_from_round_1.minor_task7_guard_clauses: ADEQUATE — Task 7 description now explicitly states: 'Guard: no full pipeline, no mockups, no interview, no new components.'

findings.detail: Architecture section 'Shared resources' explicitly states 'None.' — but shared/design-references/ is a cross-skill resource that Tasks 3, 4, and 5 all depend on (design-system-init, design-generate, and design-review all read from it). This creates a direct contradiction between the Architecture narrative (which describes shared/design-references/ as a key component) and the Shared resources subsection (which denies its existence). An implementing agent reading the Shared Resources section would receive incorrect signal that no coordination is needed for this resource. Per review criteria: Architecture lists multiple components using the same resource but Shared Resources subsection is empty → severity major.

findings.requirement: US-27: Shared references contain color-theory.md, composition.md, font-pairing.md with concrete rules
findings.detail: Decision 7 correctly maps color-theory.md → color-psychology.md + color-principles.md (in shared/design-references/) and composition.md → grid-techniques.md (in design-generate/references/). However, font-pairing.md is placed in skills/design-system-init/references/font-pairing.md — a skill-specific location — while the acceptance criterion groups all three under 'shared references'. The split is defensible (only design-system-init uses font-pairing during interview), but creates a gap between AC wording and tech-spec structure. No Decision documents this placement choice.

summary: 28/30 requirements covered, 1 partial, 0 missing. All 5 round-1 fixes are adequate. Two new findings: (1) Shared resources subsection says 'None' but shared/design-references/ is used by 3 skills — factual contradiction, severity major. (2) font-pairing.md placed in skill-specific location while AC groups it with shared references — no decision documents this — severity minor.
